original_abstract: Transformation stands as a cornerstone of human spirituality and serves as a primary pillar within the academic study of Christian Spirituality. At its core, transformation describes the profound, life-altering shifts an individual undergoes when encountering the Divine—a process that reshapes both the inner self and how one’s faith is expressed in the world. This article situates this dynamic process within the specialized framework of Biblical Spirituality, a discipline that bridges the gap between the existential concerns of faith and the rigorous methodologies of biblical exegesis. After defining the concept of transformation and its deeply existential overtones, the discussion outlines its relationship with the biblical text. The article explores how transformation manifests within the Bible itself and how the scriptures continue to act as a catalyst for significant change in both individual lives and broader societal structures.
humanized_abstract: Transformation is foundational to most human spiritual experiences and is one of the main foci of the discipline of Christian Spirituality. In this context, transformation refers to the dramatic changes an individual undergoes when he/she encounters divine reality, and as a result, experiences a change from the inside out and in terms of lifestyle practices. As we focus on transformation in the context of Biblical Spirituality, we will explore its relationship between the existential concerns of faith and the academic discipline of biblical exegesis. We will describe transformation with its heavily existential overtones, and then examine the relationship of transformation both within the Bible as well as in the way the scriptures function in individual and social life.
